G7000x|notes_from_pm.value: TOMB OF HETEPHERES [I]. Temp. Khufu. Husband, Snefru. Son, Khufu. No superstructure. Re-burial, transferred from unidentified tomb probably at Dahshûr. Reisner Excavation. Harvard-Boston Expedition (1925-7).
G7000x|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-2 G 7000X headword block depth. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.179 (physical p.176) opens § B. EAST FIELD with `G 7000X. TOMB OF HETEPHERES [I]¹ / Temp. Khufu / Husband, Snefru. Son, Khufu. / No superstructure. Re-burial, transferred from unidentified tomb probably at Dahshûr. / Reisner Excavation. Harvard-Boston Expedition (1925-7). / REISNER and SMITH, Giza Necropolis, ii ...`. Per the chunk-2 prompt's headword-block rule, the block extends until the first bibliographic ribbon line (`REISNER and SMITH, ...`). Agent A's transcription is the longest faithful capture of this block (drops only the trailing `¹` footnote marker per the chunk-2 prompt's footnote-stripping rule); agents B and C each truncated short. Selecting A's content as the most PM-faithful capture of the full headword block.

LG84|occupant_alt_names.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-3 LG 84 PAKAP alternate-name normalisation. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.290 headword reads `LG 84. PAKAP good name WEHEBREc-EMAKHET Overseer of scribes of the King's repast, etc. Dyn. XXVI.`. The all-caps trailing-`c` (in pypdf) is PM's raised-ayin glyph for Late-Period Greek-derived throne-names like Wahibre (Apries) — same typographic class as chunk-1's `MENKAUREa` rendered with raised-`a` and resolved to `Menkaureʿ` (U+02BF). Three agents produced three different normalisations: `Wehebrec-Emakhet` (Agent A; kept the `c` literal + capitalised `Emakhet`), `Wehebrec-emakhet` (Agent B; kept `c` + lowercase post-hyphen), `Wehebreʿ-emakhet` (Agent C; converted `c` → U+02BF ayin + lowercase post-hyphen). Agent C's form is the most Egyptologically-faithful: the `c` is the raised-ayin and should be the canonical ayin glyph (matches chunk-1's `Menkaureʿ`); the post-hyphen `emakhet` is the locative `m-akhet` ("in the horizon"), not a proper name, so lowercase. Selecting Agent C.

SAQ-IputII|notes_from_pm.value: PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI.
SAQ-IputII|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-IputII headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.432 (physical p.72) prints `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT [II]¹` (regnal-number bracket + footnote marker) on a line followed by `Dyn. VI` on the next line. pypdf concatenated the lines and rendered `[II]¹` as `Il1`. Three agent forms: `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI.` (Agent A; regnal-number normalised + sentence-final period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT. Dyn. VI` (Agent B; missed the regnal number entirely and missed the period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI PYRAMID.` (Agent C; correct regnal + concatenated the next sub-heading `PYRAMID.` from the body block). Agent A is the most PM-faithful: regnal number rendered correctly per the chunk-4 bracket-regnal rule + sentence-final period preserved + headword block correctly bounded at the `Dyn. VI.` line (does NOT spill into the body's `PYRAMID.` sub-heading).

SAQ-Neit|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-Neit headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.431 (physical p.71) prints `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT` on a line followed by `Dyn. VI` on the next line, then `PYRAMID.` as a body sub-heading. Three agent forms: `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI.` (Agent A; sentence-final period preserved), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI` (Agent B; missed the period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI PYRAMID.` (Agent C; concatenated the next body sub-heading). Same pattern as SAQ-IputII: Agent A is the most PM-faithful — sentence-final period preserved + headword block correctly bounded at `Dyn. VI.` line. Selecting A.

SAQ-MerenreI|notes_from_pm.value: I. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF MERENREʿ I. Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXIX; Perring and Vyse, 8.
SAQ-MerenreI|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-MerenreI headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.425 prints `MERENRĒʿ I` (macron-ē + raised-ayin); pypdf rendered the macron-ē-plus-ayin cluster as `£c`. The conventional Egyptological transcription is `MERENREʿ I` (restoring the `E` vowel of the `Re` element; parallel to chunk-1's `Menkaureʿ`). Three agent normalisations: `MERENREʿ I` (Agent A; restored the `E`), `MERENRʿ I` (Agents B and C; strict-rule dropped the vowel). Selecting Agent A's form per egyptologist-reviewer F1 P1 — the `Merenreʿ I` conventional form is the Egyptological standard. The parallel `fix_rows.py` CHUNK4_CORRECTIONS entry restores the `occupant_name` to `Merenreʿ I` so all fields stay internally consistent. Also picks `Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. ...` (with period before PYRAMID) over Agent C's concatenated form.

G1151|notes_from_pm.value: Overseer of the department of tenants of the Great House, Prophet of Khufu: Prophet of Reʿ in the Sun-temple of Neuserreʿ, etc. End of Dyn. V or later.
G1151|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1151 KEDNUFER headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.56 (physical p.53) prints `G 1151. KEDNUFER Overseer of the department of tenants of the Great House, Prophet of Khufu: Prophet of Reʿ in the Sun-temple of Neuserreʿ, etc. End of Dyn. V or later.`. Three agent variants: (A) colon `Khufu:` + properly normalised ayin `Reʿ`/`Neuserreʿ`; (B) semicolon `Khufu;` + ayin normalised; (C) colon + un-normalised `Rea`/`Neuserrea` (raised-`a` left literal). The pypdf text-layer extraction shows COLON (verified at line 303 of chunk-6 text file), so agents A and C have the correct punctuation; the chunk-wide ayin-normalisation rule (raised-`a` → U+02BF) means agent A's `Reʿ`/`Neuserreʿ` is the correct normalisation per the source-wide convention established in chunks 1 + 3 + 5 (Menkaureʿ, Wehebreʿ-emakhet, Userkaf-aliases etc.). Agent A's form is the only one matching BOTH the text-layer punctuation AND the source-wide ayin convention. Selecting A.

G1221|notes_from_pm.value: Shad (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.
G1221|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1221 SHAD headword block. The pypdf text-layer extraction (chunk-6 file line 432) reads `G 1221. SHAD (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` — the doubled `(?) (?)` is an OCR-stutter artifact, NOT what PM prints (printed-source verification: PM III.1 p.59 prints `SHAD (?)` once, per the egyptologist-reviewer pass). At merge time, before the egyptologist had verified the printed source, the three agent variants were: (A) `Shad (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (full text-layer-verbatim form, preserving the stutter); (B) `Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (dropped the name); (C) `(?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (kept the hedges, dropped the name). Selecting A at merge time as the most text-layer-faithful form (captures the candidate name). The subsequent fix_rows.py CHUNK6_CORRECTIONS entry then trims to `Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` per the egyptologist's recommended form once printed-source verification confirmed the OCR stutter. This two-step path makes the OCR/printed-source delta auditable.

G1201|notes_from_pm.value: King's son, Prophet of Heqet, heka-priest of Meḥyt, etc. Temp. Khufu.
G1201|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1201 WEPEMNEFERT headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.57 (physical p.54) prints `G 1201. WEPEMNEFERT King's son, Prophet of Heqet, heka-priest of Meḥyt, etc. Temp. Khufu.` — only Meḥyt (the cobra-goddess of Thinis, transliterated mḥyt) gets the underdot-Ḥ; Heqet (the frog-goddess, transliterated ḥqt) appears in PM's printed text without the diacritic per the volume's typesetting convention. The pypdf text-layer renders Meḥyt's underdot-Ḥ glyph as a mid-word uppercase `H` (`MeHyt`), parallel to chunk-3's underdot-glyph rendering issues. Three agent variants: (A) `Meḥyt` (correctly normalised to underdot-Ḥ); (B) `Mehyt` (dropped the underdot, lossy); (C) `MeHyt` (left the raw OCR uppercase-H, non-canonical). Agent A is the Egyptologically-faithful transcription matching PM's printed glyph. Selecting A. Pattern parallels chunk-3 LG 84's `WEHEBREc-EMAKHET` → `Wehebreʿ-emakhet` underdot normalisation. Egyptologist printed-source pass verified the asymmetric Heqet (no diacritic) vs Meḥyt (with diacritic) treatment matches PM's actual typesetting.
